62edc5411249c9578b2abe02.leadin: In 1992, a poet born in this country relocated from Jamaica to the US state that titles his 2019 poetry collection Nebraska. For 10 points each:
62edc5411249c9578b2abe02.leadin_sanitized: In 1992, a poet born in this country relocated from Jamaica to the US state that titles his 2019 poetry collection Nebraska. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this country. An author from this country who died in a 2013 shooting in Kenya allegorized colonialism as a bird “preaching salvation to us that owned the house” in “The Weaver Bird.”

The author of Nebraska, Kwame Dawes, is the editor of Prairie Schooner, a prestigious one of these institutions. Other examples of these institutions include Granta, The Kenyon Review, and The Paris Review.
Kofi Awoonor’s poem “Lament of the Silent Sisters” is dedicated to this poet, who wrote “Elegy for Alto” shortly before being killed in the Nigerian Civil War.
